textify(Yr||(Yr=(0,C.Z)([" The second Frostwyrm probes your mind with some intensity, searching your memories for the clutch you had raised with [frostwyrm.name]. You can’t see what it sees; your interaction with it as it rifles through your head is an alien and somewhat uncomfortable feeling of pressure in some nebulous, undefinable part of your mind. It feels like it’s in the center, yet it has a gravity that pulls on the edges and contracts the surface of your brain towards it. And it does so unevenly, with one probe pressing in on you from the front, and another squeezing in from your right. If [frostwyrm.name] weren’t there to guide it, the experience would go from unpleasant to borderline torturous. A few moments later (which felt significantly longer than that), all of the pressure releases, and your mind is your own again. You look to the second Frostwyrm for its expression, to see if it’s satisfied with what it found – only to see that its head has reared back, with all four of its ruby-red eyes peeled wide. Its nostrils flare as it goes from you to [frostwyrm.name][frostwyrm.andNykke | to Nykke ] and then back to you. That was your clutch? it asks, as if it had not seen for itself through your own memories. Still, you confirm. And that was your nest? Large enough to hold such a clutch? Again, you confirm – and, in fact, it could hold much, much more. The second Frostwyrm’s voice in your head goes silent. Its body remains tense, each of its muscles wound up as it considers what it has just seen and what you’ve just told it. You turn to [frostwyrm.name] and ask her what a typical clutch is for a Qim and a Qal. You don’t really have a basis for how large it ‘ought’ to be. Two, she answers. Three is uncommon, but not unusual. ... Your clutch is significantly larger than that. Indeed it is.[frostwyrm.andNykke | “For reference, I was an only child,” Nykke adds. ] Why don’t, or, didn’t, Frostwyrms have larger clutches? You’d have gathered that, with how much she enjoys mating, larger Frostwyrm families would have been much more common. There are a multitude of reasons. Anything from food insecurity, to nests not being large enough to sustain more, to the nest being in a precarious location, susceptible to attacks from lesser creatures hoping to poach the kips as they incubate. The location of my nest – in the ocean, away from poachers, in a hollow island, and surrounded by limitless fish to eat – nullifies all concerns. She lowers her snout to you, her throat trilling as she affectionately nuzzles against you. And if I am particularly libidinous, it is because you make me so, my Qal. My kind do not typically mate for the pleasure of it, and yet, with you... I could not ever return to that way of thinking. The second Frostwyrm continues to sit there and stare at you, gobsmacked at everything it’s seen and everything it’s hearing. And, as you consider what [frostwyrm.name] just said, even from a non-Frostwyrm standpoint, she’s making your arrangement sound awfully attractive: food security; a massive nest; no poachers trying to snatch her eggs; and, apparently, all the mind-numbing sex a gigantic, twenty-foot-tall, forty-foot-long dragon could ask for. You take the opportunity to press the point: if there’s anything it doesn’t believe, it’s free to search [frostwyrm.andNykke |the memories between all three of you |your memories, or [frostwyrm.name]’s ]. It’s already seen the size of your clutch and it can infer the size of [frostwyrm.name]’s lair; perhaps it doesn’t believe that the ocean is large enough to feed a massive family of dragons. Maybe it doesn’t know how good sex can feel when it’s not for procreation[frostwyrm.andNykke |. Hell, when it’s all three of you, the sex is so mind-numbingly intense that you barely stay conscious through it, and it’s hard to know when it starts and when it ends ]. If there’s anything it’s unsure about or wants to know more of, you, as the Qal, are the teacher: it’s your job to teach. It takes the second Frostwyrm a long moment to compose itself, and when it finally does find the wherewithal to loosen its body and blink its eyes, it’s with a sort of groggy, disoriented body language that reminds you of someone who’s been asleep for a decade. And you’re offering me a place amongst your clutch? it asks you. You know that it’s not what it had in mind when it set out from its bearers’ home in search of a mate for itself. Circumstances are different and times are difficult for all of the remaining Frostwyrms. But if it can accept that it’d be sharing the duties as Qim with [frostwyrm.name][frostwyrm.andNykke | and Nykke ] – hunting for food, raising the kips, and patrolling the perimeters of the territory – then, yes, the offer to join you is there. It’s why you[frostwyrm.andNykke| three | and [frostwyrm.name] ] came out here in the first place. It takes another moment to answer, but you can feel its emotions coming to you via the psionic link. At first, something downtrodden and remorseful; this isn’t what it wanted. It wanted a traditional family: a mate of its own; a lair of its own; not having to share what it has with others. But it also knows you’re right. This is the only chance it’s had in a very, very long time to settle down and have something better than what it has now, and there’s no guarantee that, if it turns you down, it’ll ever find another Frostwyrm to be with. It’s taken so long just to find [frostwyrm.name]. Living with your clutch as another Qim is a better life than wandering the snowy wastes and hoping beyond hope that it’ll get a second chance – and that’s assuming the unlikely scenario that another Frostwyrm isn’t already taken. You soon feel a second set of emotions: this time, something more akin to that light, flighty feeling you get in your upper chest when you start to hope that something good might happen. The opportunity is there – it just has to seize it.[frostwyrm.andNykke | “If it’ll help make up your mind,” Nykke says, putting her lower hands on her hips and giving them a flaunty swish, her tail arcing through the snow and kicking up a line of it. “I get where it is you’re coming from. [frostwyrm.name] found [pc.name] before I did. I was left wandering the world for hundreds of Sky Cycles before I finally found them both. And I was caught wanting the same things you wanted: a normal life. I’m not a pureblood, so I was worried that I wouldn’t make for a good Qal. But my scales also make it so I can’t blend into the environment, meaning I struggled to hunt, so I wouldn’t make a good Qim, either. Being with [pc.name] and [frostwyrm.name] changed my life in so many ways, and all of them for the better.” The second Frostwyrm’s nostrils flare. Its eyes train on Nykke; it’s taking everything she says seriously. The nest is not yours to begin with, it notes. How do you fare with the... arrangement? “I mean, I have everything I could want. A home; kips of my own... I’m smaller than [frostwyrm.name], and that makes me more agile in the water, so I’m a better fisher than she is. But most of all, I have companionship. Something to deal with all of that crushing loneliness. I felt kind of awkward about it at first, but... there’s really nothing more that I could ask for.” Before the Frostwyrm has the chance to further consider its options, Nykke continues. “And the sex – the mating. Good Grand Qim, it is life-changing. I just wanted something to deal with all the heats and the ruts, but what I got....” She steps behind you and wraps her arms around you, hugging you to her – and sinking your head into her cleavage. And you can feel the bulge of her vent pressing up against [pc.heightRange 0 60 84 108 |your head, casting you into shadow |your upper shoulders, near the back of your neck |your lower back, and quickly riding upwards |your butt, sinking into the valley between your cheeks ]. “Trust me when I say that nobody in this world fucks like [pc.ra frostwyrm |[pc.name] can |[pc.race]s can ]. [frostwyrm.name] will agree with me.” It tilts its head in confusion. ‘Fucks?’ it asks. What is this word? You had expected it to dig into your head for its definition, like [frostwyrm.name] likes to do whenever you use a word she doesn’t understand, but [frostwyrm.name] beats you to the punch. Although you don’t understand the exchange, you can tell that they’re communicating – and after a moment, the second Frostwyrm rears back slightly in surprise. I understand. ] It takes a while longer for the Frostwyrm to deliberate with itself whether or not to accept the invitation. Eventually, with a heavy sigh that flares its nostrils, it bows its head. I accept, it says. Given its tone, it had to chew through those two little words – after all, this isn’t the life it wanted, but it’s the one it’s getting. This scenario is unfortunate and unusual for us all, but it is not just me that must accede to the realities of our world. Your offer is munificent and I will not overlook it. In the end, you managed to solve things amicably: you’ve found the second Frostwyrm, and you’ve managed to convince it to join your clutch back in [frostwyrm.name]’s lair.[silly | And all it took was for it to see just how capable you are at making babies with dragons. ] "])))
Next

textify(Sr||(Sr=(0,C.Z)([" The second Frostwyrm probes your mind with some intensity, searching your memories for the clutch you had raised with [frostwyrm.name]. You can’t see what it sees; your interaction with it as it rifles through your head is an alien and somewhat uncomfortable feeling of pressure in some nebulous, undefinable part of your mind. It feels like it’s in the center, yet it has a gravity that pulls on the edges and contracts the surface of your brain towards it. And it does so unevenly, with one probe pressing in on you from the front, and another squeezing in from your right. If [frostwyrm.name] weren’t there to guide it, the experience would go from unpleasant to borderline torturous. A few moments later (which felt significantly longer than that), all of the pressure releases, and you’re left alone in your mind again. You look to the second Frostwyrm for its expression, to see if it’s satisfied with what it found – and it doesn’t look all that impressed. Frostwyrm faces aren’t as expressive as [pc.race]s, but you can tell from its body posture and the tilt of its head that it expected more. Perhaps, by traditional standards, it is an impressive clutch, it admits. Though it is as you say: we live in more trying times. From the stories you had spun, I was expecting a clutch of epic proportions – a clutch to rival the progeny of the Grand Qim. [frostwyrm.name] lets out a long sigh, her body visibly deflating. Our kind are not usually so prone to hyperbole. The second Frostwyrm turns its head, eyeing you with its two red eyes. I have also watched your encounter together, albeit from the point of view of the mongrel. You can see its pupils – as well-blended as they are with the red sclera – are blitzing across your body, watching your every detail, from the contours of your muscles to the reflexive twitches you make when you know you’re being watched. I have been in countless battles myself. Every scar and deformity on my body is a testament to them all, and I have emerged victorious in each and every one. Well, yeah, of course it did. It’d probably be dead if it ever lost. The second Frostwyrm snorts in derision at your observation. Your mate was chosen based on your combat prowess. It stands to reason, then, that the better combatant between us ought to have the right to mate. Wait a minute, nobody said anything about fighting! And what about [frostwyrm.name] – doesn’t she get a say in this? Even if it wins against you– Before you have the chance to finish the thought, the second Frostwyrm spreads its wings with enough force to kick up a blinding white gale of powdered snow, blanketing you[frostwyrm.andNykke |, Nykke, ] and [frostwyrm.name]. The high winds, the frigid cold, and the sting of the snow’s dust against your [pc.skinFurScalesNoun] disorient you; you feel your [pc.feet] lift off the ground and you lose your balance, and then, the second Frostwyrm flaps its wings back down, creating what feels like a snowstorm – a real force of nature. You use your mind to try and reach out to [frostwyrm.name][frostwyrm.andNykke | or Nykke ], to see if [frostwyrm.andNykke |they |she ] can pinpoint you, but you don’t get any reply. Instead, you feel something akin to a force, or maybe a hand, pressing down on the back of your mind, as if it were trying to stem a flow of water. You’ve been psionically attuned to the second Frostwyrm, and now it’s using that against you! Our battle will be of the same conditions as the battles between you and your mate, the second Frostwyrm’s distinct ‘voice’ says in your head. With another flap of its gargantuan wings, you see its silhouette in the swirling powder: its head lowered slightly, with its wings spread apart from its body but folded somewhat defensively against itself, giving it a larger, more rounded shadow. No mates[frostwyrm.andNykke |, or others, ] to turn the tide of battle. To the victor goes the right to mate. Although your psionic link with [frostwyrm.name] has been blocked, you can still feel every emotion coming from the second Frostwyrm, clear as could be. There’s everything from adrenaline and excitement at the prospect of getting into another fight... but there’s also something else. A type of unease; a sort of desperation that you’d expect from someone who felt like they were at the end of their options. After all, you never knew [frostwyrm.name] to be an unreasonable creature. It’s a fight! "])))

textify(xr||(xr=(0,C.Z)([" It may have been some time since you last did combat with [frostwyrm.name], but your body remembers all of it. The motions, the techniques, what to watch out for – it’s all very similar. Whatever doubts this second Frostwyrm had about your combat prowess is surely assuaged after that final blow. Yet... for all its strength and combat experience, you can tell that it was holding itself back. When you fought [frostwyrm.name], she fought with intent: you were an intruder in her territory and she fought tooth and claw to keep you out. When this one swiped at you with a claw, it was with less strength than you know it’s capable of; when it snapped its jaw at you, it was more for the spectacle than to land a bite. It lies half-buried in the snow, panting from the pain and the exertion. You’re still psionically attuned with it, and you don’t feel any malice or indignation towards you. In fact, you don’t feel much of anything: a hollow, vacuous space exists in your mind where you expected it to project its emotions. When it catches its breath and rolls itself over to lie on its stomach, you ask why it attacked you. It takes a long, deep sigh, in and out through its nose. Similar to [frostwyrm.name] and her sighs, it lasts nearly a full minute. Pride, it answers simply, with a downward lilt in its voice, like it knew it was defeated... but not necessarily by you. I could not accept that an outsider would have taken[frostwyrm.andNykke | not one, but two viable mates | the only remaining viable mate in this part of the world ]. There is no deeper meaning to my actions. As it speaks, the weather anomaly surrounding you two dissipates. The snowy winds get weaker and weaker, returning visibility to the snowy plains around you – and the next thing you hear is [frostwyrm.andNykke |a pair of wingbeats as your two Qims, quick to find you now that the psionic block is down and the weather is quieting, hurry to your side |a heavy set of wingbeats as your Qim, quick to find you now that the psionic block is down and the weather is quieting, hurries to join your side ]. Despite all the excitement, what you feel from [frostwyrm.name]’s connection with you isn’t panic or anger or anxiety – she’s rather calm and collected. And she’s moving with all the grace and ease of a woman taking a walk through her garden. [frostwyrm.andNykke |They are |She is ] quick to deduce the results of your confrontation. After all, you’re still standing, and it isn’t. You’ve proven your mettle once again over another Frostwyrm. And you feel something else through your link with her: confidence, bordering on smugness. She knew from the beginning what the outcome was going to be. When she lands, she leans her snout towards you, inspecting you with her four ruby-red eyes (just to confirm that you had won as handily as she knew you would) before gently nuzzling into you, a light purr emanating from her throat. There was no doubt in my mind, she trills.[frostwyrm.andNykke | Nykke, however, doesn’t give you a look, knowing that [frostwyrm.name] has you tended to. Instead, the very first thing she does when she sees you and the second Frostwyrm is skid to a stop right next to its face – and then rear her two right fists back, punching it square in the snout, just beneath its left eyes. It flinches and winces, but does not attempt to get away. It knows it deserved that. From the empty feeling you’re getting from it, it might think it deserves more. ] You reciprocate the nuzzling and tell [frostwyrm.name] that you’re fine; you weren’t about to let another Frostwyrm get the best of you like that. After all, you were fighting for the right to keep her as your mate. I would never agree to such a cruel term, she admonishes before resuming her upright, regal posture, sitting on her rear haunches as she looks down upon the defeated Frostwyrm before you. Although it’s already beaten and prostrated in the snow, she pointedly and deliberately lifts her snout at it, forcing herself to look down. And under normal circumstances, neither would you. You are knowingly acting like a fool. The second Frostwyrm sighs again, turning its head to the right and sinking it into the snow. It’s been defeated both physically and emotionally. It knew what it did was wrong and that this wasn’t how Frostwyrms act towards one another. When [frostwyrm.name] called it a fool – and, you note, not a Qer – you felt a pang of deep sadness, the kind where you feel a cold pit in the center of your chest, but it makes no attempt to argue in its defense. You ask [frostwyrm.name] if she intends to rescind the invitation to her lair. It’s her home, after all. It is our home, she corrects. And.... She pauses, standing stock still above the second Frostwyrm, her four red eyes boring into its every scale and watching its every bodily movement. It, in response, pulls itself upward, into a sitting position similar to her own, and with its head upright and level... but still hung lower than hers. Proud and almost defiant... but aware of its place between you [frostwyrm.andNykke |four |three ] all the same. As I said: I had no compunctions on the result of your misbegotten ‘contest.’ [frostwyrm.andNykke |Our Qal |My Qal ] is Qal for a reason. Your actions would turn the Grand Qim away from you and cast you into the world’s shadow for the rest of your life. Its posture doesn’t change, but its own four red eyes break from hers, scanning along the frozen wasteland stretching in every direction around you. Though my judgements aren’t without sympathy, she continues. We are in desperate times. Our way of life is endangered; the ways of our bearers and ancestors before us are no longer viable. Despite my Qal’s capability, [pc.heShe] was not my first choice in mate either. You tell the second Frostwyrm that she likes to repeatedly remind you of that. I do not condone your actions, though I understand them. My first compunctions towards my Qal were also violence. She breaks eye contact – or, more appropriately, she stops staring daggers into the second Frostwyrm – to turn towards you. You had asked if I intend to rescind my invitation. That is not a decision for me to make. It is our home, but you are the Qal. [frostwyrm.name] is leaving the choice of whether you should take this second Frostwyrm into her lair to you. Keeping in mind everything that’s happened so far, from the state of the Frostwyrms to how this one in particular acted towards you (and the remorse it feels for doing so), your choice is– "])))
Vouch
context: <<POS:01300366>> addButton(e++,"Vouch",yh,"Vouch","This Frostwyrm was confused, desperate, and lonely. Wandering the world for years on end, thinking you’re alone, would make anyone act foolishly. This one doesn’t deserve exile; it just needs a chance.")

original: textify(Ar||(Ar=(0,C.Z)([" Your spirit may still be willing – you’re fighting not just for your own pride, but for the right to keep [frostwyrm.name] as your mate, at least according to this stranger of a Frostwyrm. But your body can’t keep up, and after that last strike, you feel the grip leave your fingers and the stability leave your knees. With a lurch, you collapse in the snow, your hands sinking down to your forearms into the powder. With a thunderous clap, the second Frostwyrm lands beside you, its heavy body kicking up enough snow to half-bury you beneath its wave. You hear a growl emanate from its throat as it lowers its head to you, about a body’s length away; it knows that you’ve been beaten and that the fight is over. A new feeling comes over you via the psionic link you have with it. An undeniable wave of relief; the kind that makes you relax from the base of your neck all the way to the bottom of your spine. It contrasts sharply against the dull, throbbing pain that the rest of your body is feeling. You ask it if it’s going to kill you. You had beaten [frostwyrm.name] many times, and you never finished her. And when she finally got the upper hand, she reciprocated the gesture. I will not, it answers. This was never a fight to the death. You then ask if it really intends to try and take [frostwyrm.name] as its mate. This was never a contest that she agreed to. You don’t know if this is a real thing that Frostwyrms do – challenge each other for the ‘right’ to mate with another – but you know how [frostwyrm.name] feels about you, and you doubted from the start that this would be something she’d ever be okay with. To that, the Frostwyrm doesn’t answer. It becomes visibly apprehensive; you hear its breath catching in its throat before it holds it altogether. Its foremost right foot rakes through the snow, leaving long divots where its claws mark through it. And through your psionic link, you feel a mix of emotions, but two of them are stronger than the others: nervousness... and shame. Before it has a chance to formulate its thoughts, you hear the sound of air being cut as something slices through it – or, more appropriately, as something batters its way through it. Something huge and lumbering, moving at a high speed. Before either of you have the chance to register what it was, [frostwyrm.name] barrels into the second Frostwyrm, the whole of her weight colliding into it. She doesn’t land claws-first; there’s no doubt in your mind that she would have skewered the Frostwyrm in two if she had. They tumble through the snow, one overtop the other, for several yards until [frostwyrm.name] ends up on bottom with her legs pressed against the second Frostwyrm’s torso. With a powerful kick from all six legs, she sends the second one flying through the air, tumbling over itself from the momentum. By the time they’re on their feet, both are covered, head to tailtip, in snow. [frostwyrm.name] stands between you and it, her head whipping back and forth repeatedly to make sure that you’re still behind her. Her wings flap and her tail thrashes back and forth with agitation: her whole body is tense and ready to strike at a moment’s notice[frostwyrm.andNykke |. And soon to join her is Nykke, her own wings flapping twice as hard to keep up, although she doesn’t immediately join [frostwyrm.name]; rather, she goes straight to you, making sure you’re still alive before putting herself between you and [frostwyrm.name] ]. The Grand Qim would weep at the sight of you, [frostwyrm.name] says, although her voice comes to you a little foggy. The second Frostwyrm’s psionic block must still be in effect, although it’s losing its focus after that attack from [frostwyrm.name]. The very idea of taking a mate of one from another. I had never dared to think that there were levels beneath that of a Qer, and yet you have found them. The second Frostwyrm staggers to its feet, limping on the side that [frostwyrm.name] had tackled it. Its breath comes out ragged and strained from the pain. With belabored effort, it stands up on shaky knees, lifting its head to face [frostwyrm.name]. You don’t need a psionic link to tell how remorseful it feels; it almost looks like a whipped puppy that knows it’s about to get it again. I will make no excuses, it says. Nor would I hear them if you had! [frostwyrm.name] barks back. From her body language, she’s just a moment away from lunging forward and ripping its heart out with her teeth. My empathy for you has been exhausted; your innards remain inside you solely on my charity. If I ever see you in my territory again, it, too, will be reduced to nothing! This is the most animated you’ve ever seen [frostwyrm.name]; you don’t remember her being this excited when she faced off against you so long ago. Her words, her posturing, and her demands all have their effect on the second Frostwyrm; despite having won the battle against you, it’s not about to try and fight [frostwyrm.name][frostwyrm.andNykke | and Nykke ]. Although, it makes you wonder what the whole ordeal was even for. Why had it challenged you to a fight if it wasn’t going to try and take [frostwyrm.name] from you to begin with? Suddenly, you feel what can only be described as a ‘yank’ in the back of your mind, and the psionic link that you had with the second Frostwyrm is severed. You’ll never have the chance to ask it now. Acting as though it’s an innocent animal about to be locked in a cage, the second Frostwyrm, with its head hung low and its tail totally limp, turns away from you and spreads its wings. With one heavy flap, it lifts its massive body into the air and it takes off into the sky, aiming towards the horizon to the north. You don’t doubt that that’s the last you’ll ever see of it. [frostwyrm.andNykke |Nykke remains on guard as it flies away, her arms tensed and ready and her claws out – but as soon as the second Frostwyrm crosses the horizon and disappears, she turns around and goes back to you. “Are you okay?” she asks as she skids to her knees in front of you, her four hands scouring your body for your injuries. “Are you okay? Tell me you’re okay!” You’re fine – you’re just a little beat up. Everything is still where it’s supposed to be. In all honesty, your pride took a bigger hit than your body: you had beaten [frostwyrm.name] in a fair fight before, but you couldn’t do it again against this stranger. “Who cares? [frostwyrm.name] and I sure as hell don’t!” Nykke pulls you upright with two hands, using her other two to pat along your back to feel for any more injuries. “By the Grand Qim, as soon as we lost you in that blizzard, [frostwyrm.name] and I nearly had a heart attack!” ]Once the second Frostwyrm is out of sight, [frostwyrm.name] turns back toward you, stomping through the snow to get to you with her own shaky legs. You can tell that the adrenaline is still flowing through her body enough to keep her off-balance. Do not spare another thought to that Qer, she says, and from the tone of her voice, that was an order. That was no child of the Grand Qim. That was an impostor made to look like one. As far as [frostwyrm.andNykke |any of us |we ] are concerned, there has been no sighting of another of my kind anywhere in the vicinity. This isn’t up for debate, and [frostwyrm.name] isn’t in the mood to be reasoned with, especially if it’s in favor of your attacker. That second Frostwyrm is gone from your life forever, now. Still, a part of you hopes that it’ll find a mate of its own someday. [frostwyrm.name] knows better than anyone what a life of solitude can do to someone. "])))

textify(Nr||(Nr=(0,C.Z)([" You don’t know a lot about the Grand Qim other than it’s an important figure in Frostwyrm mythology, almost to the point of religion. But from the way [frostwyrm.name] talks about it, there’s one thing in particular that you’ve gleaned: that it’s a very parental figure that only wants the best for all of its many children. Maybe [frostwyrm.name] is right in that the Grand Qim would be ashamed of how this other Frostwyrm had just acted, but you doubt its first choice would be punishment – it’d be compassion. To try and understand the confused fury building within it and to help quell it. Not with more anger, but with an extended hand... or, claw, rather. She’s said it [frostwyrm.andNykke |twice before, with Nykke and now with this other Frostwyrm |before ]: she knows what it’s like being lonely and thinking you’re the last surviving member of your race for years of your life. [frostwyrm.andNykke |Her exact words when you were talking with her about Nykke were that she ‘would never wish another of her kind to suffer the same tortures.’ |She even said that she would never wish that sort of fate on anyone, let alone another Frostwyrm. ] Still, there’s the fact that this Frostwyrm, after extending an invitation to it like you had, chose to attack instead. You don’t believe it’s violent; it’s confused. The living situation of the Frostwyrms is complicated and uncomfortable. But it had admitted to you that it attacked out of pride – because it believed that you didn’t deserve to be [frostwyrm.name]’s mate. You’ll need to do something about that pride before you let it into [frostwyrm.name]’s lair, where your clutch is. You tell the second Frostwyrm that you’re still willing to let it in. Better that the Frostwyrms keep together than to splinter off; there’s so few of them left. But if it does, it needs to accept that you are the Qal. It will have food and shelter and a place to hunt, but it will answer to you. You’ve proven yourself capable twice over, now. And it also needs to accept that things aren’t going to be like it thought they would be. You are [frostwyrm.name]’s mate. It will be sharing a lair with [frostwyrm.andNykke |two other Frostwyrms, and all three of you |another Qim, and both of you ] are Qims to you. It’ll be segregated from the rest of your clutch until you and [frostwyrm.name] are comfortable with it being there. Still sitting with its head hung low, it sighs, flurries of snow kicking up around its nostrils as it exhales. That same, hollow feeling of resignation from before has never left. You can guess that this wasn’t how it imagined today going: it found another Frostwyrm, only to learn that it was mated outside of their species, and then it got its ass kicked. Being told that its ideal family life isn’t possible would also hurt. But the alternative is for it to go back to the wilderness and try its luck again for the rest of its life. You tell it that you’re offering it a home; a family (or, perhaps more accurately, a roommate. Anything more comes later); food and shelter; and, most importantly, something to help with its crippling loneliness. It won’t be what the wyrm always imagined it would. But if it’s willing to try, then it might find that what you’re offering it instead is better. After all, [frostwyrm.name] likes to tell you that you weren’t her first choice in mate, but she also likes to say that if she knew then what she knows now, she wouldn’t have chosen anyone else. Besides, it’s not like you’re not taking a huge risk with extending this offer. Inviting it into the lair is a vulnerable position for you and [frostwyrm.name]. Hopefully it appreciates the gravity of the scenario you two have put yourselves in for its sake. The Frostwyrm’s eyes stay on you through the whole speech. As downtrodden as it is after having lost the fight and getting the lashing it did, you have its undivided attention. Once you finish, it lifts them to [frostwyrm.andNykke |Nykke first, looking over her halfbreed form, then to ][frostwyrm.name], watching her and her body language. It doesn’t say anything, and you don’t feel any new feelings from your psionic link with it. It’s taking the proposal seriously. Eventually, it lifts its head, trying to match [frostwyrm.name]’s posture. Seeing them stand side-by-side like this, with similar poses, you notice that the second Frostwyrm is just a little bit smaller than her. I accept, it says. Given its tone, it had to chew through those two little words – after all, this isn’t the life it wanted, but it’s the one it’s getting. I cannot apologize for my actions, nor would I expect you to forgive me if I had. I can only promise that I will do better. [frostwyrm.name] holds still for a moment, considering what it had just said. After a tense moment, she lets out the air in her lungs, her body deflated before you two. Be sure that you do. "])))
Next
Next

textify(Er||(Er=(0,C.Z)([" On the one hand, while you don’t agree with the motivations this other Frostwyrm has for acting the way it did, you at least understand where it’s coming from. It’s confused; lonely; and the only viable mate it’s found since leaving the clutch of its own bearers was already taken by you, a [pc.ra frostwyrm |Frostwyrm-in-name-only |non-Frostwyrm ]. But that doesn’t excuse its actions. It knows that; that’s why it didn’t apologize. You had extended an invitation for it to join your clutch – a highly vulnerable position for you and [frostwyrm.name] – and it reacted aggressively. You can’t, in all reason, let it into your home with your mate and your kips. Even if it only acted out of confusion and desperation, that still sets a bad precedent: will it react badly if there’s a food shortage? Will it get mad and confused if it sees your half-breed Frostwyrmlings in the lair? What if it just wakes up on the wrong side of the bed one day? You had come here to extend the invitation to another Frostwyrm to live with you[frostwyrm.andNykke |, Nykke, ] and [frostwyrm.name] as a part of your family, but that was before all of this. You have your own family to look after. You can’t allow this Frostwyrm near your kips, and you tell it as much. It’d be best for everyone if it left and resumed its search for its own mate once again. The Frostwyrm doesn’t move, still silently on its haunches with its head hung low. It’s four red eyes close, hidden behind white eyelids. It’s still projecting its emotions into you, and that cold, hollow feeling in your chest deepens, like it knows that a tragedy is unavoidable. Without another word, it turns on its spot, showing its back to you and [frostwyrm.name], before taking a few steps through the snow. Once it’s about a dozen Frostwyrm-sized paces away, its massive, leathery wings unfold, and, with a flap, it takes off into the air, kicking up a cloud of snow as it lifts. After another moment, you feel what can only be described as a ‘tug’ in the back of your head, and any emotion you feel from the second Frostwyrm is disconnected. It’s gone for good. [frostwyrm.andNykke |Both [frostwyrm.name] and Nykke approach you, with [frostwyrm.name] on your left and Nykke on your right. textify(Or||(Or=(0,C.Z)([" We will leave for my lair, [frostwyrm.name] says as she pulls herself into a standing position. All haste is necessary.|The chill of Light’s Fall affects us all, and the journey is not short; we must make haste.] The second Frostwyrm watches you curiously as you go to mount [frostwyrm.name]’s shoulders. Just as you put your hands on the broad swell of her shoulder, where her sides meet her wings, you stop. There’s one last thing you want to establish with the second Frostwyrm before you leave. My name is– As is typical, its voice repeats a serious of syllables you can’t reproduce with your mouth – although you do notice that it sounds very distinct from [frostwyrm.name]’s. I take this to assume you’d like to choose a new name for me, it asks. Is that alright? It lifts its eyes to [frostwyrm.name], regarding her for a moment. A thick, broad form is described as ‘manly.’ A common colloquialism in your language to encourage one acting cowardly is to tell them to ‘man up[silly| And I resonate well with this culturally-significant hero you know as ‘He-Man].’ Every scar on my body is a testament to the encounters I have survived; I feel it appropriate that my identity be associated with the pronouns that your culture implies resolve and sturdiness. textify(Mr||(Mr=(0,C.Z)([" [flurry.name] is a pureblood Frostwyrm, just like [frostwyrm.name] is. He is extremely large: from his feet to the horns on his head, he stands at about twenty feet, and that’s not even including the fold of his wings. He has six legs: two where you’d expect a normal quadruped’s would be, and two more in between them, sprouting out from closer to the center of his torso. He’s covered in pure-white scales that help him blend into the snowy wastes of Uveto, although the underside of his body and the inside of his legs are a dull gray. Each scale is hard enough to withstand strikes from any conventional weapon – it’d take some concentrated fire to really hurt him. Like [frostwyrm.name], his feet end in razor sharp claws, each of them capable of rending through metal with enough force. Lining the inside of his mouth are two sets of teeth, layered so that one set is in front of the other; he has enough jaw strength that he could chomp a steel beam in two. His long, thick tail is capped with a set of spikes, each of them longer than your arm, but they’re not sharp like his talons are – they’re much better for bludgeoning than for piercing. In his skull are a quartet of ruby-red eyes with darker dots in their centers to denote his pupils, and each of them are a little smaller than your fist. Sprouting from his back are a pair of massive, leathery wings that, when unfurled, give him a combined wingspan of forty feet, and capable of generating enough lift for flight. However, these are all surface-level observations that apply to any Frostwyrm. [flurry.name] differs from [frostwyrm.name] in a number of ways. Where [frostwyrm.name] is covered in swirling-blue designs across her body, [flurry.name] has straighter lines that reach higher across his body, nearly up to his back – you’re not certain of the significance, but it may be a way for Frostwyrms to visually discern one from another, almost like fingerprints. [flurry.name] is also covered in scars, primarily along his flanks and underside, but with a few across his snout and several along the membranes of his wings. None of them are animalistic in nature; they’re more reminiscent of primitive weaponry, like spears, arrows, and bolas. The claws on his front-right and middle-right feet have been broken into blunt tips. There are several spikes that are outright missing from his club tail, leaving divots where they used to be. He’s missing the tip of his tongue. Like any Frostwyrm, hidden between [flurry.name]’s rear flank are a pair of external testicles, packed into a tough, leathery scrotum. [flurry.throbb |They were already massive before, but after you gave him that shot of Throbb, his balls have gotten so large that he needs to keep his thighs spread to keep from pinching them together. Each incredible nut is so massive that, if you stood behind him and he squatted down, you’d be absolutely crushed, head to toe, under the sheer immensity of his draconic balls. textify(Fr||(Fr=(0,C.Z)([" It may be a difficult topic to talk about, but you ask [flurry.name] if he’s willing to talk about the scars all over his body. He has injuries that range from long, deep gashes along his flanks and thighs, to holes that have been punctured in his wings. He’s even missing whole parts of himself: some of his claws are missing, and the spikes on his tail are blunted and a few have been chipped off. Each scar has been accrued via combat, he explains. Every injury is a testament to my tenacity and perseverance. It is my obduracy that has kept me alive before I had met you and [frostwyrm.name]. From the look of the scars, though, they don’t appear to have been made from other animals. These aren’t teeth or claw marks – they look like they were made with weapons. Something more primitive, like spears and arrows. [flurry.name] lifts his wing and inspects his flank, eyeing some of the scars riding up his leg as he tries to recall where he got some of the more specific injuries. My assailants are small in stature, though large in number. They wore the bones of my fallen kin as ornaments; some wore undressed skulls as ironic headwear. They came in a myriad of different colors. Some hunted as though they had no other purpose in life than to slay me and my kin. And.... You feel him prodding your memories, scouring your thoughts for something in particular. This weapon that you call a ‘spear’ matches the description of the tools that some of them had wielded. There are two different Uveto-native races that match a description like that. Does he know why he was being hunted? I had no mate, no nest, no eggs to poach. I avoided settlements where they had congregated. My food sources were larger animals found in the wastes. I often asked myself the same question, my Qal – followed by asking myself what more I could do to earn my peace. It seemed that anywhere in the world I retreated to, they would follow. You also note, though, that he’s missing chunks of his talons, and that the spikes on his tail are misshapen and some are even missing. Those must be evidence of the ‘tenacity’ he was talking about. Retreating was not always an option, he answers. They would arrive swiftly and strike when I was vulnerable – when I slept or when I ate. They would fling sharpened projectiles that would pierce my wings and make flight impossible to achieve. They would pin me under straps or nets to keep me from moving. In scenarios like those, my only remaining option was to fight for my survival. He cranes his neck to look at his rump, and he wags his tail sadly, his eyes focusing on the spots where some of the spikes should be, but aren’t. In each and every confrontation, I would trade a piece of myself for my life. For all my combat experience, my first and foremost priority is to avoid conflict... and yet, it seems, conflict always finds me. It’s clear that [flurry.name] has led a difficult life when he, like [frostwyrm.name] before him, only wanted a mate and to settle down. Frostwyrms are the apex predators of Uveto, but you’ve never known [frostwyrm.name] to be aggressive towards more sentient species like the Korgonne or the Milodan, and from [flurry.name]’s stories, he was much the same. But hey! Now [flurry.name] has a mate – more than that, he has a family. He has a Qal, and while it wasn’t his initial plan, now he has [frostwyrm.andNykke|not just one, but two other Qims to call his own and share his life with|another Qim to share his life with]. He had started with nothing, and now he has more than most other Frostwyrms would ever have in their lifetimes. And now he doesn’t have to worry about being attacked or hunted in [frostwyrm.name]’s lair! [flurry.name], however, does not share your enthusiasm. If only that were true, he laments. I had done everything in my power not to attract the attention of those that would undo me, and yet, their attention I had regardless. I trust you, my Qal, and I trust [frostwyrm.name] – it was no small miracle that you brought me into your household as it is. But there is a part of me that is apprehensive that I will not one day awake to find the lair under siege by those that would have my head. From the emotions you’re feeling through your psionic link with him – namely, anxiety – he’s being serious. There’s a worrying thought that lingers within him that he’ll never find peace. You assure him: if there were ever any intruders on [frostwyrm.name]’s island, they’ll have to deal with you. This is your family in this cave, too. It’s only words, of course... but you can feel that it gives [flurry.name] some small comfort. His head cranes towards you so that his enormous chin can rest on your lap. He’s not light, but it’s not uncomfortable enough that you ask him to get up. Should fortune be with us, your words will remain only words. With that, the conversation ends with the sound of [flurry.name]’s throat lightly churring, the purrs echoing off the icy walls of the lair. "])))

textify(_r||(_r=(0,C.Z)([" You know that it’s something of a difficult topic to talk about for Frostwyrms, but it’s an unfortunate reality that he, [frostwyrm.name][frostwyrm.andNykke |, Nykke ], and you all have to confront. You [frostwyrm.andNykke |four |three ] have been very lucky with finding each other, but the truth of the matter is, there are very few remaining Frostwyrms in Uveto, and nobody has a concrete answer why. You know it’s unlikely, but you were wondering if he had any idea as to why that might be. Given my history with the other creatures of this world, my Qal, it would be safe to assume that they have been hunted to near-extinction, he answers.[pc.isAss | You take that answer to mean that he doesn’t know for certain. No. Not ‘for certain.’ ] You ask him if he knew how many Frostwyrms there were in the world before this calamity started. And if he knows when the calamity started. A typical clutch contained two kips, he answers, in something of a roundabout way. For every clutch that maintained this quota, the population of my kind remained stagnant: neither increasing, nor decreasing. A clutch with three kips was uncommon; a clutch with only one was rare – and unfortunate. Clutches with four or more was unheard of... at the time. Compared to every other kind in the world, including your own, my Qal, my kind is massive in stature. The world cannot sustain a large population of my kind. Thus, there was a quota to carefully maintain among us. [flurry.name] pauses his answer for a moment, and his head lifts upward, his eyes scanning the icy ceiling of the lair for a long while. From his body language, he seems somewhat melancholic – and wistful. There is a fable with my kind, my Qal, pertaining to the Grand Qim. The fable discloses that the Grand Qim’s wingspan was so large, it would blot out the Skylight; with one flap of their mighty wings, even the fiercest of storms would dissipate. With their breath and body, the unending chill of the world would cease, and warmth would return to the world once again. My own bearers had told me similar accounts of the grandeur of the number of my kind. Numerous enough that a congregation of us could easily darken the sky. And so on and so forth. As soon as he takes a more flippant tone, his head lowers, and his demeanor changes to something dourer and more depressed. And yet, when my bearers joined the Grand Qim, I absconded my nest to find a barren world – cold, as I was taught to expect, but also desolate and empty. The only wings within the sky were my own. Hardly enough to darken the landscape. Hmm, that brings up an interesting point. [frostwyrm.name] said she was about two-hundred ‘Sky Cycles’ old, which, if you understand the context correctly, means she’s two-hundred years old. You ask [flurry.name] how old he is, and how old he was when he left the nest. One-hundred-ninety Sky Cycles, and sixty Sky Cycles, respectively, he answers. If he’s one-hundred-ninety now, and he was sixty when he left the nest, that means that the Frostwyrms have been endangered for at least one-hundred-thirty years – well before you had arrived. Whatever’s happening to the Frostwyrms is still happening, and it’s been going on for quite a while. You suppose it’s possible that they’re slowly being hunted to extinction, since they reproduce slowly and they tended to only breed enough to meet the status quo. But that still doesn’t sound right: [frostwyrm.name] and [flurry.name] both act like this is an apocalyptic scenario rather than something that’s been going on for generations. Although, Frostwyrms very likely live longer than you[pc.ra frostwyrm | would have expected them to |r kind do ]; maybe one-hundred-sixty years is recent to them. Uveto is a big place, and if this lair is anything to go by, it’s filled with underground cave systems large enough to house whole families of Frostwyrms. Is it possible that they’ve simply retreated underground? It is possible, [flurry.name] concedes. Though admittedly unlikely. [frostwyrm.name] and I had both wandered the plains of the world for many, many Sky Cycles; it is implausible that we would have found no entrance into these tunnels where entire civilizations of my kind reside in that time. [flurry.name] heaves a heavy sigh as his head lowers to the icy floor, depressed and resigned. With all respect, my Qal: I now have my own family, and my own clutch, and my own Qal. My needs as an individual are met. I have dwelt on the possibility that there may no longer be others of my kind in the world for too long; I would instead prefer to focus on what it is I do have now. You concede – for now. Hopefully, the mystery of the Frostwyrms and their current status is something that you [frostwyrm.andNykke |four |three ] can solve someday. "])))

textify(zr||(zr=(0,C.Z)([" When you first met [flurry.name], he was understandably confused and combative over the fact that [frostwyrm.name] had chosen you as her Qal, and, since Frostwyrms are typically monogamous, that would normally mean that she was off-limits... but then, after [flags.FLURRY_VICTORY |a bit of, well, excitement |understanding just what you mean to her ], you [frostwyrm.andNykke |four |three ] had come to an understanding – and now, here you all are, in [frostwyrm.name]’s lair, with [frostwyrm.andNykke |all of them |both of them ] sharing you as their Qal. It’s not a typical arrangement for Frostwyrms, you know that. You want [flurry.name] to give you his honest opinion: how does he really feel about it all? About mating outside of his species? About sharing you with [frostwyrm.andNykke |two other Qims |another Qim ]? At first, [flurry.name] doesn’t answer. He lies on his front with you resting against the side of his stomach, strongly considering how to answer your question. There’s a certain awkwardness in the psionic link you share with him: something reminiscent of confusion and apprehension, as if he was lost. Just as you think to tell him that he doesn’t have to answer if he doesn’t want to, his words flow into your mind. As [frostwyrm.name] has told you before, you were not her first choice in mate, he starts. That’s correct. You want to ask him how many times everyone needs to remind you that, but you decide not to – and then you think that it’s too late anyway since Frostwyrms can read your mind. Hopefully that thought just went too fast for him to read. Understand, my Qal, that [frostwyrm.name] and I both come from... traditional bearers of our own. Wanting to mate within our own kind was all we knew. It was not so much a matter of desire as it was a matter of... unknowing that other opportunities were possible. That’s funny – it was [frostwyrm.name]’s idea to crossbreed with you, and she said that she knew it was possible because others had done it. Had [flurry.name] not heard of the same stories? I suppose I should not speak for [frostwyrm.name], then, he answers. But the fact remains that she and I both wanted another of our own kind as a partner, and she had ‘settled’ for you after you had proven your prowess and expertise. She and I are better educated now, and we understand that what we have now is a greater boon than if it were just us two. And to properly respond to your query: no, I was unaware that crossbreeding with a kind like yours was possible for a kind like mine. He’s really giving you the run-around on his answer. All you wanted to know was how he really felt about being in a relationship that bucks Frostwyrm traditions. There is still awkwardness, I will admit, he finally yields. My kind are not so.... His psionics prod your mind for a moment, seeking a specific word, but he’s in there for longer than usual: he’s looking for a definition to match a word rather than a word to match a definition. My kind are not so ‘imaginative’ as yours, my Qal: thinking of alternative family arrangements was incompressible. When [frostwyrm.name] said she was beholden to you, I... I simply could not process it. That my kind would mate outside of our species was not a mere fantasy – it was an impossibility. At least, that’s how I understood it at the time. And now? It would be untrue to say that no part of me still yearns for that normalcy I had sought most of my life. A lair of my own, a mate that shares my biology, and a typical clutch of two-to-three kips. A setting as such would be all I would need to join the Grand Qim feeling as though I had done all I needed in the world. [flurry.name] turns his head towards you, his four red eyes half-lidded as he regards you with affection and contentment. And yet, every statement [frostwyrm.name] has made about you has turned out correct. In every manner, you have gone above the standard of what I would have considered a ‘proper mate’ to have been. Our kips are educated. I have companionship that I would not have had with a traditional coupling. And the mating – by the Grand Qim, I had never anticipated such pleasures could possibly exist. Even if I had the opportunity – or the inclination – for a ‘proper’ mate, I am afraid that you have sexually spoiled me for anyone or anything else. [flurry.name] lowers his head until the tip of his snout is just a few inches from your [pc.chest]. With his every breath, his nostrils flex, and a warm blast of air washes over you. There is value in normalcy. And yet, there is more to the abnormal. The arrangement between you and I and [frostwyrm.andNykke |Nykke and ][frostwyrm.name] is abnormal. He leans forward until his snout gently presses against your chest, and his eyes close in content. Now that I know the value in the abnormal, I would not have it any other way. "])))

textify(Gr||(Gr=(0,C.Z)([" You realize that you’re a bit beyond the introductions, now that, well, now that you’re his Qal and you’ve already had sex, but for you and your kind, there’s a sort of ritual that goes on between two individuals looking for a longer-term relationship than a fling, and that’s called ‘dating.’ And a part of the process of ‘dating’ is learning more about each other. So, you ask [flurry.name] to tell you a bit more about his history: he’s lived a long time and he’s been all over the world in search of a mate. Surely he’s got some interesting stories to tell. [flurry.name] doesn’t answer at first: his head rotates slightly, like a confused dog seeing its own reflections, and you feel a light grip in your head as he scours your mind for some definitions. You are speaking the truth, he says, with a somewhat surprised tone to his voice. You’re the Qal. You ask if he didn’t believe you – if he thought you were lying. Not as such, he answers. This is not a ritual that my kind perform. All relevant information on the past is brought to the forefront upon courtship. Though, I suppose, your kind do not communicate as my kind do. [flurry.name] turns his head to face toward the central dome of the lair, where [frostwyrm.name] resides. He lets out a long sigh through his nose; you watch the steam shoot several feet before disappearing into the air. I was hatched a clutch of two, he begins. My bearer Qal joined the Grand Qim six Sky Cycles after my bearer Qim. As is typical for my kind, once our bearers joined the Grand Qim, we had left the nest in search of mates of our own. [flags.FLURRY_TALK_HISTORY |[flurry.name] mentioning his parents ‘joining the Grand Qim’ reminds you of the first time you asked him about how Frostwyrms deal with their dead, and what ‘joining the Grand Qim’ means... and how he reacted badly when you told him how [pc.race]s deal with their own dead. You opt not to repeat that mistake. | You tell [flurry.name] that [pc.race]s have a somewhat similar euphemism for death: you call it ‘passing away.’ You’re curious, though: when your kind die, you usually do anything from burning the remains to ashes, or burying the body under the ground, or some combination of both. Sometimes, someone might do something different. How do Frostwyrms handle the remains of those that ‘join the Grand Qim?’ Again, you feel [flurry.name] sift through your memories, search for multiple different words and phrases that you had just used. When he’s finished, he recoils slightly, as if you had just slapped him in the nose. This practice of yours is untenable! he balks. There is no equivalent to this heinous exercise among my kind, and for good reason! You truly mean to imply that your kind treat your fallen with such odious methods?! You suppose it can be seen as barbaric, in a sense, but, on the other hand, your kind reproduce significantly faster than Frostwyrms, and your lifespans aren’t nearly as long (generally). Your kind can’t just let the bodies stay there and pile up – you’d run out of places to put them in a hurry. You understand that it might be a sudden thing for someone like [flurry.name] to realize, but you ask him, as his Qal, to trust that different species have different approaches to different issues. [flurry.name] takes a moment to collect himself as he stews on your words. Eventually, he lowers his head back to the position it was before. As you say, he relents, although his voice comes out unsteady and unfocused. To answer your question... I am uncertain. The phrase ‘joining the Grand Qim’ is not a colloquialism: it is literal. The call occurs to us each individually, at different times; when one is summoned, they leave the nest and do not return. No one of my kind truly knows what becomes of one when they are summoned. How eerie – if that’s true, they don’t even leave bodies behind for their offspring to find and figure out what happened. ] The nest of my upbringing is a massive distance away – I had traveled so far and for so long that I do not believe I would be able to locate it again. I have since come across every manner of race and beast in search of a mate until I had met you and [frostwyrm.name]. There was no trace of any other of my kind until that day: when I found the coast she had made into her roosting grounds, her scent was faint, but unmistakable. [rand |You imagine him retelling every single fight he’s ever been in would take quite a while, given the scars on his body, and they might not be memories he’d be very keen to revisit. Instead, you ask him which fight was the most memorable, in whatever sense he’d prefer: whether because the fight was very close, or because his opponent was unique, or because of some other circumstance that made it stick with him. There is one, he says immediately, knowing exactly which fight he wants to talk about. A group of four individuals, standing on their hind legs[pc.isTaur | |, not unlike yourself ], each of them wielding a sharpened tool with a length longer than their bodies. There was one alpha among them: taller and stronger than the other two, and more familiar with its tool in combat. My territory was temporary, but it was well-marked, and I would not tarry intruders. You wonder what passes for a ‘well-marked’ territory for a Frostwyrm, if these three hunters ignored the signs completely. I must include that this encounter was recent, he continues. I had much combat experience to rely on by this point. My opponents were typically smaller in stature, but larger in number; I believed that I could quell this hunting group easily. Given that he’s still here, he clearly won the fight. The alpha of the three fought alone. It stepped forward, and with a heft, it flung its tool toward me. He lifts his right wing, unfurling it above you; he points to one particular scar along its leathery membranes with his snout. It’s not very wide, but judging by its color, that weapon pierced his wing all the way through. It struck true. Fortunately, I had grown accustomed to battle injuries; this one did not hinder my performance. [flags.FLURRY_VICTORY |You imagine [flurry.name] hasn’t actually lost any fights, given that, if he did, he’d probably be dead. Any fights, that is, save for you. How did this one individual stack up to you? As you say, my Qal: this alpha among them was inferior to your skill and knowledge. He hesitates before continuing. It was, however, my most difficult encounter before you. |You imagine that this ‘alpha’ must have put up quite the fight – or, at least, displayed quite the bravado, if they were willing to fight him one-on-one and after tossing their weapon at him. The alpha was, to date, the most difficult of my encounters, he confirms. And – now that I have a clutch and a nest of my own – my last, with any fortune. ] The alpha fought with a strength I was unready for; with a ferocity matched only by my own and with a cunning foretelling of a proud and proper Qal like yourself. They had strength enough to pry my jaw apart; they had speed enough to counter my every swipe; they were nimble and endurant enough to retaliate from a thrash of my tail after it had struck true. The alpha among the trio had time and again proven its mettle – I often consider the alternative reality of the two in its entourage joining the encounter, and how blessed I am that such a reality did not befall. [flurry.name] is really talking up whoever this opponent of his was. If the match was so even and deadlocked, how did he win? By chance, ultimately, he admits. Though the egg is apart from [var1 |[flurry.name] |you ], you still have a psionic connection with the kip incubating inside it: occasionally, sensations are broadcast to you from the kip inside, but they don’t feel like communications – at least, not the same way that you and [flurry.name] communicate. They feel more primeval, and practically accidental: what you feel are raw emotions with no forethought or justification to them. Sometimes, the kip is fussy and uncomfortable, and that makes you uncomfortable; sometimes, especially if either you or [flurry.name] stray too far, it gets anxious; sometimes, you get the urge to wear a big, goofy smile on your face for minutes at a time, just because the kip feels that way, too. After the first month of incubation, the egg has become visibly larger, the shell stretching to accommodate the kip inside of it. It’s almost as if the shell itself had scales and was a part of the kip’s body rather than a protective covering that the kip is meant to break out of once it’s ready. The kip inside of it is getting much, much larger: the egg shifts and jostles with the kip’s movements inside of it, and when you put your hand to the shell’s surface, sometimes, you think you can feel the kip’s hand press back against you. By the end of the second month of incubation, the kip inside the egg was forming proper words with its psionics. Or... rather, it was forming proper thoughts to express those words. You would ‘feel’ that it’s thinking of the word ‘Qal,’ and you’d know that it was thinking of you. It’d think of the word ‘warm,’ and you’d know that it’d be asking for its Qim and Qal to cuddle against it for warmth. By the end of the third month, it was managing to put more than one thought together to form something akin to a sentence. All the while, the egg itself kept growing at a surprising rate, until [pc.heightRange 0 84 96 |it was significantly taller than you are |it became a little bit taller than you are |it nearly got as tall as you are ]. At the end of the fourth month, while you and [flurry.name] were speaking with [frostwyrm.name] on the quality of their recent catch of fish, you hear the distinct, sharp sound of an eggshell cracking apart. You and [flurry.name] rush to the egg to find that its rounded top had been pierced by what appeared to be a wingtip; with another concerted thrust from its body, the kip kicks its arms and legs, and the cracks in the shell draw much deeper along its oblong length, but not enough for the egg to split apart. [flurry.name] thinks to maybe try and help the kip out by breaking the shell in pieces, but you stop him: it’s important for the kip’s development that it does its very first task as a living creature by itself. It takes some time, but, eventually, the kip manages to break free by puncturing several small holes in the egg’s shell with its pointed claws and horns. After several tense minutes, your draconic kip lies on the lair’s icy floor, covered in bits of egg shell. [var0 |[var2 |For the first time since you and [frostwyrm.name] became a family, the kip that hatches from the egg isn’t a halfling – it’s a real, pureblood Frostwyrm, with [frostwyrm.name] and [flurry.name]’s genes coursing through them. It’s worked: your efforts to help conserve and rescue the Frostwyrms of Uveto are paying off, and you have the real, tangible, breathing results to prove it. |As you had expected from your conservation efforts, the kip that hatches from the egg is a pureblood Frostwyrm: a kip that doesn’t bear any sort of resemblance to you at all. It’s a true, pureblood Frostwyrm, with the same physiology as [frostwyrm.name] and [flurry.name] – but, inextricably, with you as its Qal. Once more, your efforts to save the Frostwyrms are paying off. ] Despite its size, it doesn’t have the same ferocious, implacable, imposing countenance as either of its Qims: where [frostwyrm.name] and [flurry.name] are covered in a thick, scaly hide, the new kip’s skin is so thin as to almost be translucent. Its tail has no spikes and is smooth from pelvis to tip. It has teeth, but they’re small and nubby and wouldn’t pierce a slice of bread, if it even had the jaw strength. It has four eyes, but all of them are shut tight. As is usual for your newborns, it’s blind, cold, exhausted, and its psionic link with you and its Qims is weak, but you can tell that it’s calling out to you. You get into your usual position with [frostwyrm.name] – but both she and [flurry.name] are the Qim to this one, and there they initially struggle with their instincts in getting into a proper position to coddle their newborn kip. It takes a bit of effort, but they figure it out. |Unsurprisingly, your kip with [flurry.name] doesn’t look much different from your kips with [frostwyrm.name]. Seven feet tall; two legs; four arms; a massive wingspan. It’s hard to believe something so massive is so young, but then again, it has its draconic Qim to compare itself to. Despite its size, it’s blind, cold, and exhausted; its psionic link with you is weak, though you can tell that it’s calling out for you and [flurry.name]. And, being the good parents that you are, you’re quick to place your kip between your bodies for warmth, with [flurry.name] draping his wing across you for added shelter. ] It’ll be a while yet before your kip is ready to join the rest of your clutch, never mind the world at large, but that’s not something you need to worry about right now. Right now, what’s important is that you have another addition to your family. That said, though: from your experiences with [frostwyrm.name] and child-rearing with her, you know that it’ll be another four months until its fully mature, enough to the point that it’ll be able to live on its own if needed. Your duty as the Qal is to ensure that your kip grows with the tools and knowledge necessary to live a comfortable life. It’d be wrong of you to leave your family now when they need you the most. The hardest part of raising a Frostwyrm is over: now, it’s time you fulfill your duties as Qal. "]))),[function(){return e},function(){return flurry.totalKids()>0},function(){return 0===flurry.totalPureKids()}]

original: textify(Pu||(Pu=(0,C.Z)([" Returning to your position against [flurry.name]’s belly as he lounges in his little corner of the lair is somewhat awkward, given that, just moments ago, you were pierced on his turgid [flurry.throbb|five feet of throbbing dragon meat|seven feet of pulsating dragon obelisk]. Your body is still sore and you’ll be leaking his cum for days, given his output. He, though, seems to be in good spirits: once you’ve gotten yourself comfortable beside him, he cranes his neck down towards you so that he can rub his cheek against you. A light, airy trilling resonates from his neck with enough intensity to echo off the walls; it throbs from his lungs enough to give you a bit of a massage as you lean against him. You stroke at his cheek and he leans into your touch, but he continues to prefer to nuzzle against your body. You note that he’s in an awfully good mood. You suppose that people displaying an ‘afterglow’ like his after having sex isn’t unusual. I have every reason to be, he answers, his nose pressing into your stomach. I have a home. I have a Qal. I am part of a family. And soon, our clutch will be growing in number. ... Oh, that’s right: Frostwyrms can tell when they or their partner has conceived. For a creature whose main dream in life was to have a normal family, you being pregnant with his kip is a big step in the right direction. [var0|I am unfamiliar with the gestation periods of your kind, he says. For my kind, it is one Sky Cycle between conception and when the kip is considered fully mature. Is this a similar timeframe for yours? Well... gestation periods and maturity are two vastly different things for your kind, but the short answer is that his kips will follow a typical Frostwyrm lifespan. You know that because you’ve reared kips to maturity with [frostwyrm.name] before. Ah, indeed, he says, feeling somewhat embarrassed. In my excitement, I had... forgotten. You forgive him.|It will be a short order until our egg is ready to lay, he continues, happily nuzzling and purring, almost to the point of chirping, into your abdomen. Our clutch will soon be larger. I am beside myself! Your hands run along the smooth scales of his head, petting the top and scratching along his chin. It’s hard not to feel excited with him – in part because his excitement literally feeds into your psyche with your psionic link.] Now that you are with kip, it is my duty as the Qim to see that your every need is sated and that your every comfort is addressed, he continues. My duties as hunter will double; I will see to it that our nest is properly matted and prepared; and I am, and will always be, ready to quell your every sexual urge, my Qal. Hah, well, you only just finished fucking. It hasn’t been ten minutes since you had [flurry.throbb|a fat chunk of that seven-foot pole|that throbbing five-foot cock] inside of you. But, honestly... when was the last time a little thing like a refractory period ever stopped you? Like you’ve done with [frostwyrm.name][frostwyrm.andNykke| and Nykke], you’re left with a particular choice to make: you could leave the lair and deal with the pregnancy as you would otherwise while you resume your travels among the stars... or you could wait here with him and let him be your Qim for as long as you need him to be. Frostwyrm pregnancies last four months, and it’s safe to say that that’s how long it’ll take until you get this egg out of you. Do you wait here with him the whole four months? "]))),[function(){return 0===eh.s.getStat("pregnancy/flurry eggs laid")}]

original: textify(Ku||(Ku=(0,C.Z)([" There are still things that you need to do and places that you need to be – but you knew what you were getting into when you agreed to be the Qal to not just one[frostwyrm.andNykke|, and not just two, but three|, but two] dragons. Frostwyrms are on the brink of extinction, after all; it would be irresponsible of you to go gallivanting around the cosmos when they need you here on Uveto. [flurry.name] heaves a long, heavy sigh through his nose, and you feel his head getting heavier on your lap as he relaxes on top of you. Even if you didn’t have your psionic link with him, you can tell that this news is a huge relief. He’s really looking forward to being a good Qim for you. You send a message to your ship that you’re going to be indisposed for a time, but you don’t give specifics. After that, it’s just a matter of kicking back and waiting for this egg to pop out of you.... "])))

textify(Qu||(Qu=(0,C.Z)([" If there’s any one thing you’ve learned during your time with [frostwyrm.name] so far, it’s that Frostwyrm eggs are pretty hardy. A bit of adventuring isn’t going to be a threat to your kip while it’s inside you. Are you certain? [flurry.name] asks, his eyes lifting up to meet yours. There’s an undeniable feeling of anxiety coursing through your psionic link with him as the words enter your mind. I.... You patiently wait for him to continue. I was looking forward to being a Qim, he admits. You aren’t robbing him of that opportunity out of malice or anything; you, as a [pc.race], have other responsibilities that you must tend to. Being a Qal is a full-time job! You promise that you’ll come straight back to the lair whenever you get a moment’s peace. He takes a long, somewhat-dejected sigh through his nose. I understand, he says, and although you’re sure he does, you can tell that he can’t help but feel disappointed in your decision. "])))

textify(Zu||(Zu=(0,C.Z)([" On the one hand, you’re the Qal to [frostwyrm.andNykke |three |two ] very devoted dragons: you so much as say a word and [frostwyrm.andNykke |any |both ] of them are at your total beck and call, willing to give you whatever it is you need. Whether it’s food; warmth; sex; or just some companionship; you have everything you could need while [flurry.name] leaps at every opportunity to provide for you, as a proper Qim ought to. On the other, though: not only does that mean the next four months of your life will lack any sort of challenge, it also makes things routine and boring. You’re barely allowed to leave the lair – you’re given supervised visits to the island’s surface whenever you want, but they won’t take you past the shoreline – and although they’re willing to sate your urges whenever you need, they won’t do anything more imaginative or physically demanding like you know a pair of dragons with cocks the size of adult [pc.race] bodies are capable of. And, to be honest, you’re kind of sick of the taste of fish. You’re the Qal: if you really put your foot down and made some demands, you know they’d acquiesce. If you really, really wanted to leave, they wouldn’t stop you. They do what they do because they care about you and your kip. But that really only makes your cage just a little more gilded, as it were. By the end of the second month, you had begun showing: your stomach rounded out and your body began to go through some obvious changes, particularly to your gait as you go from walking across the lair to waddling. Life isn’t any more difficult, largely in part because [frostwyrm.andNykke |your dragons |[flurry.name] and [frostwyrm.name] ] won’t let it be, but it does get a little more awkward. Life progresses fairly slowly: the next two more months are more of the same, with your body changing a bit more with each passing day and your stomach getting rounder and rounder. Although, your stomach does feel a little tight as the egg gets larger: something you attribute to the fact that Frostwyrm gestation is typically much faster than [pc.race] gestation. The kip developing inside of you is capable of some rudimentary psionics: it can broadcast emotions and does so at random times with random intensities, often without any warning or forethought to them; it’s almost as if the kip isn’t aware that it exists and is broadcasting emotions to you and [flurry.name] in spite of that. By the end of the fourth month, it’s getting harder to walk and the weight makes getting up or lying down a bit of a chore. Sleeping against [flurry.name] helps alleviate the stress a little bit, and his psionics help block whatever discomforts you have, but the reality is that you have a baby-sized egg inside of you that’s ready to come out any day now. Eventually, that day arrives: with an awkward jerk inside of your womb, you feel the instinctive need to lay the egg and get it out of you. Thankfully, the egg isn’t any larger than a typical [pc.race] baby, and its smooth shell and shape make it easier than you had expected to pass – but none of that is to suggest that it’s easy. It’s an arduous process that’s made slightly easier by [flurry.name] blocking your pain psionically. By the end of the ordeal, you’re left with an oblong Frostwyrm egg – and a bit of baby fat that you need to burn off. Frostwyrm gestation involves both of the parents being present for the egg’s development cycle: without both its Qim and Qal present, the egg will simply not hatch unless both you and [flurry.name] are there to incubate it. Hatching the egg will take another four months. It’s not like the egg will be going anywhere until it’s ready, but you could spend some more quality time with your [frostwyrm.andNykke |three |two ] Qims.... "])))
Unfortunately, all that time waiting produced no eggs...
